title: No atmospheric sky effects visible — world appears gray/colorless
description: When running the game, the sky appears gray or black instead of showing a blue sky with Rayleigh scattering from the Bevy Atmosphere component. The world looks colorless despite Atmosphere::earthlike(), ClearColor::BLACK, and DistanceFog all being configured.
reproduction: Run `cargo run --features bevy/dynamic_linking`. Look up — sky should be blue with sun-driven scattering but appears flat gray.
suspected_cause: Possible causes: (1) Atmosphere component requires specific plugin ordering or camera HDR mode not being satisfied at runtime, (2) ClearColor::BLACK may not be interacting correctly with the atmosphere shader, (3) DirectionalLight (Sun) may not be positioned/rotated correctly for the atmosphere to compute sky color, (4) ScatteringMedium asset may not be loaded before camera spawn.
resolution: Root cause: compute_sun_direction() used a body-frame rotation model that kept the sun permanently on the horizon (Y=0). Replaced with standard astronomical solar position formula using observer latitude (default 45°N). Sun now arcs from sunrise (6AM, 0°) to noon (45°) and sets properly.
notes: Code investigation shows all components are present: Atmosphere::earthlike(medium), ScatteringMedium::default(), ClearColor::BLACK, DistanceFog, Bloom::NATURAL (for HDR).
AtmospherePlugin is auto-registered by PbrPlugin — no extra plugin needed.
Atmosphere requires HDR on the camera — Bloom::NATURAL should satisfy this but verify at runtime.
Try running with RUST_LOG=bevy_pbr=debug to check for atmosphere shader warnings.

Fixed orbital model: replaced body-frame rotation matrix with standard solar position formula (altitude = arcsin(sin(φ)sin(δ) + cos(φ)cos(δ)cos(H))). Added compute_sun_direction_at_latitude() for latitude-aware computation.

title: Deformed terrain geometry — visible surface distortion
description: Terrain surfaces appear visually deformed or warped. The Surface Nets mesh output may have incorrect vertex positions, normals, or quad connectivity causing visible distortion.
reproduction: Run the game and observe terrain at various distances. Look for warped/stretched surfaces, especially at chunk boundaries and LOD transitions.
suspected_cause: Possible causes: (1) LOD stride mismatch — when LOD level > 0, Surface Nets uses stride = 2^level which coarsens geometry; transitions between LOD levels may create visible seams, (2) Chunk boundary stitching may have edge cases where neighbor snapshots are missing (treats boundary as air), (3) Noise values at terrain surface may produce ambiguous isosurface crossings, (4) Vertex normal computation may be wrong for steep terrain.
resolution: Two-part fix: (1) Neighbor invalidation — chunks re-mesh when neighbors arrive. (2) Continuous density field — Voxel.density encodes signed distance to terrain surface, enabling Surface Nets sub-voxel vertex interpolation instead of binary cell-center placement.
notes: Surface Nets implementation has proper boundary handling via 6 face-neighbor snapshots.
LOD hysteresis (12% dead-band) should prevent thrashing but may not eliminate all visual artifacts.
Integration tests validate chunk stitching (meshing.rs:1114, 1401) — issue may be data-dependent.
Check if deformation correlates with LOD transitions by temporarily disabling LOD (force level 0).
Two changes: neighbor invalidation in chunk_manager.rs + mesh deferral in meshing.rs. 2 new unit tests added.
Added density: f32 to Voxel struct (24 bytes, at size limit). terrain_density() helper computes smooth gradient. All 3 terrain generators (flat, spherical, planetary) set density.

title: Degenerate geometry visible inside terrain
description: When looking at terrain from certain angles or through gaps, degenerate/invisible geometry is visible inside the terrain volume. This suggests the meshing algorithm is generating internal faces that should be culled, or there are holes in the surface mesh revealing interior artifacts.
reproduction: Run the game. Fly through terrain (G for fly mode) or look at terrain edges/overhangs. Internal geometry may be visible as flickering faces or z-fighting artifacts.
suspected_cause: Possible causes: (1) Surface Nets generating quads for internal isosurface crossings (e.g., cave surfaces inside solid rock), (2) Backface culling not enabled — internal faces visible from outside, (3) Octree subdivision creating surface artifacts at resolution boundaries, (4) Chunk overlap regions producing duplicate geometry.
resolution: Same root cause as TERRAIN-001. Continuous density field eliminates staircase artifacts from binary voxel sampling in Surface Nets.
notes: Surface Nets should only generate surface mesh at the air/solid boundary. If internal geometry exists, the algorithm may be finding false surface crossings.
Check if StandardMaterial has cull_mode set to Back (Bevy default is Back).
Cave carving may create internal air pockets that generate visible but unreachable surfaces.
Octree leaf-level differences could create T-junctions or cracks.
Linked to TERRAIN-001 fix.

title: In-game map (M key) does not open
description: Pressing M during gameplay does not open the in-game map overlay. The map system (src/map/) was implemented with M key toggle, Tab for local/global switch, and ESC to close, but none of these appear to function at runtime.
reproduction: Run `cargo run --features bevy/dynamic_linking`. During gameplay (Playing state), press M. Map overlay should appear but does not.
suspected_cause: Possible causes: (1) toggle_map system may have a .run_if() condition that prevents it from running in the Playing state, (2) GameState transition from Playing → Map may not trigger OnEnter(GameState::Map), (3) KeyCode::KeyM may not match the physical key on all keyboard layouts, (4) MapPlugin may not be registered or may conflict with another input handler consuming the M key.
resolution: Fixed discovery tracking (Local<bool> initial scan guard) and confirmed map toggle works. Map overlay displays correctly.
notes: Code investigation shows MapPlugin IS registered in main.rs.
toggle_map system listens for KeyCode::KeyM with just_pressed().
System has .run_if(in_state(GameState::Playing).or(in_state(GameState::Map))) — verify this compiles and runs.
Add temporary debug logging to toggle_map to confirm the system is executing.
Fixed: track_discoveries now does a full scan on first run (Local<bool> guard), no longer relying solely on is_changed() which may miss chunks loaded during Loading state.
Added: debug!() logging to toggle_map for runtime diagnostics (enable with RUST_LOG=the_dark_candle::map=debug).
Added: 4 unit tests for toggle_map logic (Playing→Map, Map→Playing, ESC close, M ignored in other states).
If still not working: run with RUST_LOG=the_dark_candle::map=debug to see if toggle_map fires, check if game reaches Playing state.

title: Function keys not working or not matching documentation
description: Function keys (F1–F12) either do not respond or produce unexpected behavior compared to what documentation describes. Known bindings: F3 (debug overlay), F5 (ECS state dump), F9 (toggle chunk borders), F11 (fullscreen), F12 (screenshot).
reproduction: Run the game. Press F3, F5, F9, F11, F12 individually. Compare behavior to README.md and diagnostics docs.
suspected_cause: Possible causes: (1) F12 has DUPLICATE screenshot_system functions — one in diagnostics/mod.rs:260 and one in planet/render.rs:615, which may cause conflicts, (2) Some F-key systems may only run in specific GameStates (e.g., only Playing, not WorldCreation), (3) Desktop environment (Wayland) may intercept certain F-keys before they reach the application, (4) DiagnosticsPlugin may not be registered or may have incorrect run conditions.
notes: Code investigation confirmed: F3, F5, F9, F11 are all properly bound in diagnostics/mod.rs.

F12 duplicate: NOT a runtime conflict. planet/render.rs::screenshot_system is only registered via run_globe_viewer() in src/bin/worldgen.rs — a separate binary. DiagnosticsPlugin is NOT added to the worldgen app. The two functions never coexist in the same running app. It is pure code duplication (cosmetic quality issue), not a functional bug.
Wayland compositors (Sway, Hyprland, etc.) may capture F-keys for their own bindings — check compositor config.
Test with WINIT_UNIX_BACKEND=x11 to rule out Wayland-specific key capture.
Remaining open concern: Wayland key capture. All F-key bindings in DiagnosticsPlugin look correct in code.

title: Game crashes during play
description: The game crashes at some point during gameplay. Specific crash conditions, timing, and error messages not yet documented.
reproduction: Run `RUST_BACKTRACE=1 cargo run --features bevy/dynamic_linking` and play until crash occurs. Capture the full backtrace.
suspected_cause: Possible causes: (1) terrain.rs:385 has a fragile unwrap() on env.as_ref() — logically safe but could panic if use_advanced flag gets out of sync, (2) Chunk loading/meshing may panic on edge cases (out-of-bounds voxel access), (3) Asset loading race conditions (accessing materials/textures before they're loaded), (4) Stack overflow from deep recursion in octree operations.
notes: CRITICAL: Need user to provide backtrace from `RUST_BACKTRACE=1 cargo run --features bevy/dynamic_linking`.
terrain.rs unwrap() (now line ~455): verified safe — env is always Some(...) in the use_advanced=true branch (set at line ~432). Not a crash candidate.

GPU 'Buffer is already mapped' race: mitigated. generate_batch() is dispatched from a single async task at a time — v2_update_chunks checks gpu_tasks_q and falls back to CPU if a GPU batch is already in flight. Each pipeline owns its own staging buffer. No concurrent staging buffer access.
No other unsafe unwrap() patterns found in hot paths (Update/FixedUpdate systems).
Original suspected causes are now ruled out. Issue stays open pending a real backtrace — may have been fixed as a side effect of recent rendering overhaul or may not reproduce anymore.

Related: three_bouncing_balls test revealed two physics-adjacent bugs (now fixed): (1) amr_fluid injection systems required Res<TerrainGeneratorRes> unconditionally — changed to Option<Res<>> so they gracefully skip when no terrain is loaded; (2) PlanetConfig::default() uses Planetary mode with 32km radius, causing catastrophic gravity for entities near world origin — added TerrainMode::Flat for headless physics tests.

title: LOD mesh positions not scaled to chunk space
description: LOD meshes (stride > 1) output vertex positions in cell-space [0..effective_size] instead of chunk-space [0..CHUNK_SIZE]. A stride-2 LOD mesh covers [0..16] instead of [0..32], physically shrinking the mesh to half size.
reproduction: Run the game and move away from terrain (>128m). LOD chunks appear compressed toward chunk origin with gaps between them.
suspected_cause: generate_mesh_generic() computes world_pos = cx + vertex_pos without accounting for LOD stride. Missing multiplication by lod_step.
resolution: Added scale: f32 parameter to generate_mesh_generic(). LOD callers pass lod_step as f32, non-LOD callers pass 1.0. Position calculation: (cx + vertex_pos.x) * scale. Added bounding-box test.
notes: Pre-existing bug — less visible with binary density (all vertices at cell centers).
Became obvious after continuous density field (4754dbc) shifted vertices within cells.
Test: lod_mesh_spans_full_chunk_size verifies meshes at stride 1, 2, 4 span [0..CHUNK_SIZE].

title: Material color fallback table has wrong IDs for materials 4-7
description: Both material_color_fallback() functions in lod.rs and meshing.rs had wrong color assignments for MaterialIds 4-7. IRON(4) showed green, WOOD(5) showed yellowish, SAND(6) showed dark brown, GRASS(7) showed tan/beige.
reproduction: Start game before MaterialRegistry loads from RON. Initial terrain meshes show wrong colors (grass is tan instead of green).
suspected_cause: MaterialId constants were reordered at some point but fallback color tables were not updated to match.
resolution: Corrected IDs 4-7 colors to match actual MaterialId constants. Added fallback colors for IDs 12-27 (glass through copper_ore). Extended from_defaults() range from 0..=11 to 0..=27.
notes: MaterialRegistry RON files have correct colors — sync_color_map_from_registry() eventually overrides fallbacks.
But there's a startup race: first meshes may use wrong fallback colors before registry loads.
RGB values sourced from assets/data/materials/*.material.ron for consistency.

title: Camera horizon tilted at 45° on spherical planet at 45° latitude
description: In spherical terrain mode, the player spawns at 45° latitude but the camera always treats global Y as 'up'. The local surface normal at 45° latitude is (cos45°, sin45°, 0), creating a 45° mismatch between the camera's horizon and the actual ground plane. The ground appears tilted by the same angle as the player's latitude.
reproduction: Run `cargo run --features bevy/dynamic_linking` with spherical terrain mode (default). Observe the ground is tilted ~45° from the camera's horizon.
suspected_cause: camera_look() applied yaw/pitch around global Y/X axes instead of the local surface-normal frame. spawn_camera() used Vec3::Y as the looking_at up hint. Fly-mode vertical movement also used global Y.
resolution: Three fixes in src/camera/mod.rs: (1) camera_look() now prepends Quat::from_rotation_arc(Vec3::Y, local_up) as a base orientation in spherical mode, aligning camera Y with the radial surface normal. (2) spawn_camera() passes the local surface normal as the looking_at up hint. (3) Fly-mode Space/Shift use local_up instead of Vec3::Y. Added local_up_from_position() helper reused by all three systems. 6 new unit tests.
notes: Gravity and movement tangent-plane projection were already correct — only the camera orientation was wrong.
Quat::from_rotation_arc has a singularity at the south pole (local_up ≈ -Y) but this is an acceptable edge case.
The fix applies only in spherical mode; flat mode behavior is unchanged.

title: Atmosphere radii mismatch — sky horizon wrong on small planet
description: Atmosphere::earthlike() sets bottom_radius=6,360,000m (Earth) but our planet is 32,000m. The shader thinks the camera is deep inside a massive planet, causing wrong sky horizon and scattering.
reproduction: Run game in spherical mode. Look at horizon — sky scattering looks wrong, horizon doesn't match planet curvature.
suspected_cause: Atmosphere::earthlike() hardcodes Earth radii. Need custom Atmosphere with bottom_radius=32000, top_radius=92000 (planet + 60km atmosphere).
resolution: Replaced Atmosphere::earthlike(medium) with custom Atmosphere { bottom_radius: mean_radius, top_radius: mean_radius + 60000 } in spawn_camera when in spherical mode. ScatteringMedium::default() 60km coefficients are preserved.
notes: Bevy hardcodes atmo_y=Vec3::Y in prepare_atmosphere_transforms — LUT is always Y-up. Can't fix without Bevy PR. But correct radii fix scattering math.

title: Flat terrain on spherical planet — noise config too weak
description: height_scale=32m on a 32km planet (0.1%) makes terrain invisible. No NoiseConfig in planet_config.ron, legacy 2-layer blend with continent_freq=0.005 in radian-space produces nearly constant noise.
reproduction: Run game — terrain appears completely flat, no mountains or features visible.
suspected_cause: height_scale too small. continent_freq/detail_freq in radian-space need to be ~1.0 for visible features. NoiseConfig not configured.
resolution: Increased height_scale to 4000m (12.5% of radius). Added full NoiseConfig with radian-scaled frequencies (fbm_base_freq=1.0, ridged_base_freq=1.5, selector_freq=0.5, micro_freq=15.0). continent_freq=1.0, detail_freq=3.0.

title: Degenerate mesh normals point global Y on spherical planet
description: compute_normals() falls back to [0,1,0] for degenerate triangles. On a sphere, this is wrong — the fallback should be the radial direction from the planet center.
reproduction: Run game in spherical mode. Observe terrain spikes/lighting artifacts along chunk boundaries where normals collapse.
suspected_cause: Hardcoded [0,1,0] fallback in compute_normals doesn't account for spherical geometry.
resolution: Added spherical:bool parameter through generate_mesh_generic → compute_normals. When spherical, degenerate fallback uses normalize(vertex_position) instead of [0,1,0]. Threaded from dispatch_mesh_tasks via PlanetConfig.

title: Spherical terrain generation 125× slower with NoiseStack — per-voxel noise sampling
description: SphericalTerrainGenerator::generate_chunk() called sample_surface_radius() for every voxel (32768×) instead of caching per angular column (1024×). With NoiseStack (15 Perlin calls per sample), chunk gen took 332ms vs 2.6ms legacy.
reproduction: Run game with height_scale=4000 and noise: Some(...) in planet_config.ron.
suspected_cause: No column caching for surface_radius in spherical generate_chunk. Each voxel recomputes noise even though lat/lon varies <0.001 rad within a 32m chunk.
resolution: Pre-compute surface_radius for each (lx,lz) column at chunk mid-Y, reuse for all Y levels. Reduced noise calls 32× (32768→1024 per chunk). Chunk gen: 332ms→90ms.
notes: Remaining time after column caching was dominated by Perlin::new() inside all noise functions — fixed in PERF-003.

title: Shell loading centered on mean_radius — no chunks load when height_scale > shell_depth
description: desired_chunks_spherical() computed shell bounds as mean_radius±128m. With height_scale=4000, camera spawns at r≈33265 (actual surface) — outside the shell. Zero chunks loaded, game unresponsive.
reproduction: Set height_scale=4000 in planet_config.ron, run game. Camera spawns on surface but no terrain loads.
suspected_cause: Shell centered on mean_radius instead of camera radial position.
resolution: Centered shell on camera's current radial distance (cam_r) with extra margin of 5% height_scale. Shell now tracks player position regardless of terrain height variation.

title: Perlin::new() called ~340K times per chunk — permutation table rebuilt on every noise evaluation
description: NoiseStack methods (fbm, ridged, selector, warp, micro, continent) and terrain helper functions (ore_material, strata_material, is_multi_scale_cave, is_crystal_deposit) all created fresh Perlin objects inside their hot loops. With ~15 Perlin creations per NoiseStack::sample() × 1024 columns + ~10 per underground voxel, each chunk built ~340,000 Perlin objects. Each Perlin::new() initializes a permutation table from the seed.
reproduction: Run game with NoiseStack enabled in planet_config.ron. Game freezes on startup due to chunk generation consuming all CPU time.
suspected_cause: Perlin objects created inside evaluation loops instead of being cached at construction time.
resolution: Cached all Perlin objects at construction time: NoiseStack stores 14 Perlin fields (fbm×8, ridged×8, selector, warp×2, micro, continent, ocean_floor), CachedGeologyPerlin stores 10 (strata, ore×4, cave×4, crystal). Both TerrainGenerator and SphericalTerrainGenerator hold a CachedGeologyPerlin. Chunk gen: 90ms → 5ms (18× speedup).

title: GPU compute noise acceleration for terrain surface radius evaluation
description: Surface noise evaluation (NoiseStack::sample()) for terrain height consumes CPU thread pool time that could be used for meshing, underground voxel fill, and physics. Offloading the 2D column noise to GPU compute frees CPU cores and achieves 93× speedup for surface noise specifically.
reproduction: N/A (enhancement, not bug).
suspected_cause: Surface noise is embarrassingly parallel (1024 independent columns per chunk). GPU hash-based Perlin executes all columns in parallel.
resolution: Added GpuNoiseCompute pipeline (noise_compute.rs + noise_eval.wgsl). ChunkManagerPlugin auto-initializes GPU on startup (graceful fallback via GpuContext::try_new()). update_chunks batches up to 8 chunks (8192 columns) per GPU dispatch. Benchmark: 433µs per 8-chunk batch, 54µs per chunk. Phase 1: surface noise only; underground geology (caves, ore, strata) stays on CPU.
notes: GPU uses hash-based f32 Perlin vs CPU permutation-table f64 — terrain is NOT bit-identical but statistically comparable (GPU std/CPU std ratio ~2.6).
Phase 2 (future): underground 3D noise on GPU — accounts for 95% of total noise evals but requires porting material classification logic to WGSL.

title: V2 greedy mesh Y-axis faces transparent due to wrong winding
resolution: Flipped winding order for axis==1 in emit_quad(). The (u,v)->(X,Z) mapping creates a left-handed frame for the Y axis.
reproduction: Run with --pipeline v2 --scene spherical_planet. Top faces of all voxels are invisible.

title: Thousands of 'Resource does not exist' errors when running V2 pipeline
reproduction: Run game with --pipeline v2 (or default). Console floods with 'Resource does not exist' errors every frame.
resolution: Changed all non-optional Res<ChunkMap>/ResMut<ChunkMap> usages outside V1-internal modules to Option<>. Four systems fixed across commits 7973486 and 8c9e9cf.

title: Player falls through terrain geometry outside planet mode
reproduction: Start a game in a non-planet mode (e.g. flat/local terrain). The player entity falls through the terrain mesh instead of colliding with it.
description: Flat/local terrain mode removed (2025 refactor). All terrain is now planetary.
resolution: V2 pipeline uses analytical height-field collision in camera_gravity() via ground_height_from_terrain_gen() + WorldPosition, with sea-level clamping. resolve_collisions() in collision.rs is intentionally a no-op stub (V1 mesh collision removed). Verified: camera_gravity samples terrain surface radius analytically, preventing the player from going below ground regardless of loaded chunks.

title: HUD / UI invisible during Playing state
reproduction: Start game with --planet flags. HUD, crosshair, hotbar, and all UI elements are invisible despite being spawned as ECS entities.
resolution: Root cause: DirectionalLight shadow camera (no Camera3d, no CameraRenderGraph) and player camera both had order=0 targeting Window(Primary). Without IsDefaultUiCamera marker, Bevy UI camera selection was ambiguous. Fix: added IsDefaultUiCamera component to the player Camera3d spawn in src/camera/mod.rs.

title: Seed always 42 regardless of --seed CLI argument or preset selection
reproduction: Run with --seed 999 --scene spherical_planet. Terrain is identical to --seed 42.
resolution: Two bugs: (1) apply_planet_config_from_asset overwrote CLI config with planet_config.ron (seed=42). Now skips override when SkipWorldCreation present. (2) UI preset selection didn't rebuild terrain generators. Added rebuild_terrain_on_config_change with Bevy change detection.

title: bodies/injury.rs uses Bevy 0.14 EventWriter/EventReader and Event derive
reproduction: cargo check --tests fails with 'cannot find type EventWriter/EventReader' in injury.rs
resolution: Changed #[derive(Event)] to #[derive(Message)], EventWriter to MessageWriter, EventReader to MessageReader, and add_event to add_message. Fixed E0507 move-out-of-borrow by matching &region.tier. Fixed in Phase 10 skeleton implementation.

title: bodies/plant.rs uses Vec<Transform> in Deserialize asset (Transform lacks serde)
reproduction: cargo check fails: Transform does not impl Deserialize
resolution: Changed Vec<Transform> to Vec<super::skeleton::BoneTransform> which is serde-compatible. Test code updated to use crate-absolute path.

title: bodies/locomotion.rs references RayHit.pos field which does not exist
reproduction: cargo check fails: no field pos on RayHit, available fields are x,y,z
resolution: Changed hit.pos[1] to hit.y

title: steam.material.ron: boiling_point None prevents steam from ever condensing
description: steam.material.ron had boiling_point: None. The state_transitions.rs condensation check requires boiling_point to be Some(...). Because it was None, steam never condensed back to water regardless of temperature, breaking the steam_condensation simulation scenario and any game mechanic relying on steam→water transition.
reproduction: Run cargo test --test simulations -- steam_condensation. Steam at 450K in a stone box never condenses even after thousands of ticks.
suspected_cause: Data entry oversight: boiling_point field left as None instead of Some(373.15) when the material was authored.
resolution: Fixed boiling_point: None → boiling_point: Some(373.15) in steam.material.ron. All simulation scenarios now pass.
notes: condensed_into: Some("Water") was already correctly set; only boiling_point was missing.

title: lava.material.ron: melting_point None prevents lava from ever solidifying to stone
description: lava.material.ron had melting_point: None. The state_transitions.rs frozen_into check for Phase::Liquid requires melting_point to be Some(...). Because it was None, lava never solidified back to stone regardless of temperature, breaking the lava_cooling_to_stone simulation scenario and any game mechanic relying on lava→stone solidification.
reproduction: Run cargo test --test simulations -- lava_cooling. After 50000 ticks, 81 lava voxels remain despite being well below 1473K solidification temperature.
suspected_cause: Data entry oversight: melting_point field left as None instead of Some(1473.0) when the material was authored.
resolution: Fixed melting_point: None → melting_point: Some(1473.0) in lava.material.ron. Lava now solidifies to stone at 1473K.
notes: frozen_into: Some("Stone") was already correctly set; only melting_point was missing.

title: Spherical planet terrain appears flat at ground level due to wrong noise frequency units
description: In spherical terrain mode, noise is sampled as perlin.get([lon * freq, lat * freq]) where lon/lat are in radians. The SphericalPlanet preset and NoiseConfig defaults use frequencies tuned for flat terrain (world-meters), not radians. With mean_radius=32000m, 1 radian = 32km at the surface. fbm_base_freq=0.005 produces features at 1/0.005 * 32km = 6400km wavelength, while the entire planet circumference is only ~201km. From ground level (visibility ~500m), the terrain is completely flat because all noise features are thousands of km wide. micro_freq=0.15 gives 213km features with only 3m amplitude -- undetectable at ground level. The frequencies need to be scaled by mean_radius (roughly 2000x) to produce ground-level terrain variation.
suspected_cause: Flat-terrain NoiseConfig defaults (fbm_base_freq=0.005, ridged_base_freq=0.008, micro_freq=0.15) are reused unchanged for spherical terrain, but spherical terrain samples noise in radians while flat terrain samples in meters. Correct frequencies for a 32km planet: fbm_base_freq ~2.0-5.0, ridged_base_freq ~3.0-6.0, micro_freq ~100-200 (for ~200m ground-level features), warp_strength ~0.1-0.3 radians.
resolution: Surface terrain now uses PlanetaryTerrainSampler (IDW from tectonic PlanetData) instead of SphericalTerrainGenerator FBM noise. Noise frequency mismatch no longer affects surface terrain appearance. SphericalTerrainGenerator is retained only for internal cave carving in PlanetaryTerrainSampler.

title: Bevy Atmosphere shader incompatible with spherical-world coordinates -- sky appears black in planet mode
description: Bevy's built-in Atmosphere component (bevy::pbr::Atmosphere) is designed for flat/local worlds where camera.Y = altitude above planet surface. Its get_view_position() shader function computes: world_pos = camera_world_position + vec3(0, bottom_radius, 0). In flat mode this correctly gives r = bottom_radius + altitude. But in spherical planet mode the camera is at 3D position e.g. (22628, 22628, 0) (radius 32001.7m from origin). After the Y offset: world_pos = (22628, 54628, 0), r = 59100m, simulated altitude = 27100m. At 27km simulated altitude the Rayleigh optical depth is ~0.002 (near-vacuum), so the sky is essentially black with only the sun disc visible.
suspected_cause: Bevy Atmosphere shader adds (0, bottom_radius, 0) to camera world position, assuming flat-world convention (Y = altitude). For spherical planets where |camera_pos| = bottom_radius + altitude, this offset displaces the camera to an incorrect position in atmosphere space.
resolution: Skip Atmosphere component in spherical mode. Added update_spherical_sky system that computes zenith sky color via CPU Rayleigh scattering (sky.rs) and sets ClearColor. Boosted ambient light by 2.5x in spherical mode to compensate for lost IBL. Atmosphere still used in flat mode.

title: V2TerrainGen not updated when PlanetaryData changes
description: rebuild_terrain_gen_if_planetary() updates SharedTerrainGen and TerrainGeneratorRes but does not update V2TerrainGen (SphericalTerrainGenerator in V2WorldPlugin). Planet config changes applied at runtime or via apply_planet_config_from_asset do not propagate to the V2 rendering path. This means --planet mode always uses default noise parameters regardless of PlanetaryData.
suspected_cause: SphericalTerrainGenerator lacks a new_planetary() constructor. V2WorldPlugin initialises its own generator independently. Introduced during V1 pipeline removal.
resolution: Changed V2TerrainGen to hold Arc<UnifiedTerrainGenerator> instead of Arc<SphericalTerrainGenerator>. Updated generate_v2_chunk to accept &UnifiedTerrainGenerator (using planet_config() and sample_surface_radius_at()). Updated rebuild_terrain_gen_if_planetary to propagate the planetary Arc to V2TerrainGen. Added PlanetaryTerrainSampler::material_at_radius() delegating to its internal SphericalTerrainGenerator. Added planet_config() and material_at_radius() to UnifiedTerrainGenerator. All terrain generators (SharedTerrainGen, V2TerrainGen) now share the same Arc and are always in sync.

title: Overflow in accel_perturb_seed when plate index is large
description: The accel_perturb_seed() function used regular multiplication (plate as u64 * 0x1234...) instead of wrapping_mul, causing an arithmetic overflow panic in debug builds when plate indices exceed ~14 (possible after rifting creates new plates).
reproduction: Run tectonic simulation with Extended mode and a seed that triggers rifting (e.g., seed 42, grid level 4).
suspected_cause: Regular multiply instead of wrapping_mul in hash function.
resolution: Changed to (plate as u64).wrapping_mul(...) in both accel_perturb_seed and rift_seed. Commit: de8c7ac
notes: Pre-existing bug only exposed by the new rifting mechanic creating plate indices > 14.

title: Forward-scatter advection was a no-op due to sub-step displacement below Voronoi boundary
description: Sub-stepped forward-scatter advection used 0.5 cell-diameter CFL sub-steps, but the Voronoi boundary on an icosahedral grid is at ~0.5 cell-diameter spacing. Every cell's forward destination rounded back to itself, making advection a complete no-op (0% cell movement).
reproduction: Run tectonic simulation at any grid level with the old sub-stepping code and measure cell displacement per sub-step.
suspected_cause: Sub-step displacement threshold (0.5 cell-diameters) was below the Voronoi cell boundary distance.
resolution: Replaced sub-stepped approach with single-pass full-displacement Rodrigues rotation. Each cell is rotated by the full dt displacement, giving 98%+ cell movement.
notes: This bug meant plate advection existed in the code but had zero effect on the simulation output.

title: Cross-plate material contamination in forward-scatter advection caused all plates to merge
description: Forward-scatter advection mapped material from plate A's cells onto plate B's territory without checking plate ownership. Continental material from plate A was stamped onto plate B's oceanic boundary cells, creating continental-continental convergent contacts that triggered aggressive suturing. All plates merged into one within a few simulation steps.
reproduction: Run tectonic simulation with advection enabled at grid level 4, seed 42, Quick mode. Check plate count after simulation.
suspected_cause: 1-source and multi-source advection resolution did not filter by plate_id, allowing cross-plate material transport.
resolution: Added same-plate filter to 1-source and multi-source advection resolution. Only material from cells belonging to the same plate as the destination is transported. Cross-plate boundary interactions are handled by boundary effects (orogeny, rifting, trench descent).
notes: Combined with the sub-stepping cascade bug (TECT-004), this was the primary blocker for visible plate movement.

title: Sub-stepping caused cascading trailing-edge oceanic conversion
description: CFL sub-stepping read modified state from previous sub-steps, causing trailing-edge oceanic crust creation to propagate inward at 2 cells per sub-step. Over 5 sub-steps per simulation step and 30 steps, nearly all continental crust was converted to oceanic.
reproduction: Run tectonic simulation with seed 55 (all-continental start), grid level 4, Quick mode. Check continental count after simulation.
suspected_cause: Each sub-step snapshot read the state modified by the previous sub-step, not the original pre-advection state.
resolution: Eliminated sub-stepping entirely. Single-pass full-displacement forward scatter uses Rodrigues rotation on the unit sphere. Rotation is volume-preserving for plate interiors, so the forward mapping is approximately bijective without needing CFL sub-stepping.
notes: On a sphere, rigid rotation preserves the Voronoi mapping for interior cells regardless of displacement magnitude.

title: Concentric banding/quantization artifacts in elevation terrain
description: Deformation zones used integer BFS hop count with discrete exponential falloff (0.45^hop), producing visible concentric ring bands around convergent boundaries.
resolution: Replaced hop-based discrete falloff with continuous geodesic distance Gaussian (sigma=600km), added Laplacian smoothing passes, increased erosion to double-pass with 3% base rate.

title: Volcanic terrain shaping (edifice building + mantle plume hotspots)
resolution: Implemented volcanic edifice building in apply_volcanic_terrain() with convergent/divergent/interior build rates. Added mantle plume hotspots via init_hotspots(). Continental arc volcanism now detected. 2 new tests.

title: Wave-like periodic pulse artifacts on planet surface
description: Discrete advection shifts boundary positions each step; full boundary forces applied at new positions created parallel ridge/trench trails.
resolution: Added boundary-age temporal damping (BOUNDARY_RAMP_STEPS=5), mantle drag (MANTLE_DRAG_COEFFICIENT=0.15), and rebalanced erosion (rate 0.08, 4 passes).

title: Player spawns before terrain loads — falls through or floats above surface
suspected_cause: Loading screen check_loading_complete() monitored V1 PendingChunks which is always 0 for V2 worlds, causing immediate transition to Playing before any terrain chunks loaded. V2 chunk dispatch used arbitrary HashSet iteration order, so spawn-area chunks were not prioritized.
resolution: Three-part fix: (1) check_loading_complete now gates on V2ChunkMap loaded count >= 9 when V2TerrainGen is active, (2) v2_update_chunks sorts dispatch candidates by coord-space distance to camera so spawn-area chunks generate first, (3) snap_to_surface system on OnEnter(Playing) repositions camera exactly on the analytical terrain surface.
notes: Added V2ChunkMap::loaded_count() and V2PendingChunks::pending_count() public accessors.
Loading screen text now shows chunk loading progress.
snap_to_surface uses ground_height_from_terrain_gen() which works without loaded chunks.

title: Player spawns underwater when default spawn location is in ocean
suspected_cause: Default spawn at 45°N/0°E could be ocean. snap_to_surface and camera_gravity used ground_height_from_terrain_gen() (terrain surface only) without clamping to sea level, placing the player on the ocean floor.
resolution: rebuild_terrain_gen_if_planetary was setting terrain_res.0 to a Spherical noise generator via from_planet_config(), while shared_gen and V2TerrainGen used the Planetary tectonic sampler. Spawn position queries used the wrong elevation map. Fixed by creating a second PlanetaryTerrainSampler for terrain_res.

title: update_locomotion spams Res<ChunkMap> errors in V2 pipeline
reproduction: Run with V2 pipeline (default). Console floods with "Res<ChunkMap> failed validation: Resource does not exist" every frame.
resolution: Changed Res<ChunkMap> to Option<Res<ChunkMap>> in update_locomotion. ChunkMap is a V1-only resource; V2 uses V2ChunkMap.

title: Global map zoom only moves marker, does not scale image
description: scroll_zoom updated global_zoom state but update_global_map only applied zoom/pan to the player marker position, not the map image node. The image always displayed at 100% container size.
resolution: Added overflow:clip to container, made MapImageNode absolutely positioned, and scale/reposition it in update_global_map based on zoom and pan state.

title: Teleport pixel_to_lat_lon queried wrong node for bounds
description: map_teleport queried MapImageNode for ComputedNode bounds, but with zoom the image is larger than the viewport. Coordinates would be wrong.
resolution: map_teleport queried GlobalTransform which does not exist on Bevy 0.18 UI nodes. Replaced with UiGlobalTransform. Also updated pixel_to_lat_lon signature and all unit tests.

title: Gaps between chunks — grid-like seams vary with latitude
description: Visible gaps exist between adjacent chunks, producing a grid-like pattern across the terrain surface. The gap width appears to vary with latitude, suggesting a coordinate-space or scaling mismatch in the cubed-sphere chunk positioning or mesh generation.
reproduction: Run the game with --planet and fly around. Gaps between chunks are visible as dark lines forming a grid. Observe that gap width changes at different latitudes.
suspected_cause: Two issues: (1) NeighborSlices::empty() was always passed to greedy_mesh, causing false boundary faces. (2) The gnomonic (cubed-sphere) projection creates non-uniform angular spacing: chunks at face centers need to cover ~27% more arc length than their 32m local extent, causing 8.7m gaps. Chunks at face edges overlap by ~11.6m.
resolution: Phase 1: Changed NeighborSlices to 2D boundary slices sampled in the current chunk's own local frame. Phase 2: Added world_transform_scaled() that computes per-chunk tangent-plane scale factors from the gnomonic angular footprint. Terrain generation and boundary slices now use scaled local offsets. Chunk entity Transforms include non-uniform (scale_x, 1.0, scale_z) so meshes cover the correct angular extent. Tests verify adjacent chunk edges meet within 1m.
notes: Each voxel now covers slightly more or less than 1m depending on position (0.6m at face edges, 1.27m at face centers). This is geometrically correct — there's more surface area to cover at face centers.

title: Terrain disappears at altitude — hard view distance cutoff
description: When flying at high altitude, the terrain disappears completely beyond a fixed distance. There is no graceful LOD fallback — instead of rendering lower-detail chunks at greater distances, the terrain simply stops. Players should be able to fly at arbitrary altitude and still see terrain below, albeit at reduced detail.
reproduction: Run the game and fly upward (increase altitude significantly). At a certain height the terrain vanishes entirely, leaving only sky. Expected: terrain remains visible at all altitudes with progressively coarser LOD.
suspected_cause: V2LoadRadius was fixed at horizontal=12, vertical=2. desired_chunks_v2 used a fixed loop range and skipped out-of-range u/v coords instead of wrapping cross-face.
resolution: Implemented adaptive load radius using horizon distance formula d=sqrt(2Rh+h²). Horizontal radius now scales with altitude, capped at 48 chunks. Vertical range always extends from surface (layer 0) to camera layer plus padding. Added cross-face chunk loading via wrap_cross_face(). Surface-layer chunks get dispatch priority.
notes: Long-term: proper LOD/impostor system still needed for very high altitudes where even 48-chunk radius is insufficient.

title: Terrain collision broken after floating-origin changes
suspected_cause: ground_height_from_terrain_gen() received render-space Vec3 but lat_lon() expects absolute world position.
resolution: Changed ground_height_from_terrain_gen to accept DVec3 world positions directly. Camera systems pass WorldPosition.0 instead of render offset.

title: Map click-to-teleport places player at wrong location
suspected_cause: map_teleport() updated WorldPosition and RenderOrigin but did not sync Transform.translation.
resolution: Added Transform sync to teleport system. Set translation to Vec3::ZERO since WorldPosition == RenderOrigin after teleport.

title: LOD vertical scaling broken — chunks are thin pancakes
description: LOD > 0 chunks had only 32m vertical extent regardless of LOD level, causing terrain to appear as depressed square with staircase edges.
reproduction: Fly to altitude and observe terrain LOD rings
suspected_cause: tangent_scale.y was hardcoded to 1.0, center_r/base_r/top_r not LOD-scaled
resolution: Added lod_scale = 2^lod to center_r, tangent_scale.y, base_r/top_r, voxel Y, boundary slice Y, mesh bounding Y

title: GPU terrain produces flat/empty chunks - noise mismatch with CPU
description: After GPU voxel generation was added, terrain appeared as opaque square chunks with no sculpted geometry. Root cause: GPU used hash-based Perlin noise while CPU used permutation-table-based Perlin from the noise crate, producing completely different terrain at every point.
reproduction: Run with GPU terrain enabled. Chunks are flat squares instead of sculpted terrain.
suspected_cause: GPU ihash() function produces different gradient vectors than CPU PermutationTable lookup, leading to completely different noise output.
resolution: Replaced GPU hash-based Perlin with permutation-table-based implementation matching the noise crate exactly. Pre-compute 32 perm tables (one per noise function) on CPU, upload as storage buffer. Fixed 3D gradient function (cases 13/14 differ from classic Perlin shortcut). Fixed micro-detail inclusion, continent blend coordinates, and crystal seed mismatches. All 1635 tests pass.
notes: 3D gradient: noise crate maps case 13→(-x+y), 14→(-y+z), while classic Perlin shortcut gives 13→(-y+z), 14→(y-x)
noise_floor quirk: floor_to_isize(0.0) = -1 (not 0), replicated in WGSL
Perm table generation verified against noise crate output at multiple test points

title: Phantom camera from AmbientLight #[require(Camera)] causes dark/conflicting render
description: spawn_lights spawned (SkyAmbient, AmbientLight{...}) as a standalone entity. In Bevy 0.18, AmbientLight has #[require(Camera)], so Bevy auto-adds Camera + its required components to that entity, creating a phantom camera at (0,0,0) rendering to the primary window at the same order as the player camera. This caused camera ambiguity (black render). Additionally, update_ambient queried this phantom entity instead of updating the GlobalAmbientLight resource, so the player camera ambient was never updated from its default.
resolution: Removed the SkyAmbient entity/component. spawn_lights now sets GlobalAmbientLight resource directly. update_ambient now takes ResMut<GlobalAmbientLight> and updates it each frame. Also added safe up-hint fallback in looking_at calls when sun_vec is near ±Y.
suspected_cause: Bevy 0.18 API change: AmbientLight became a per-camera component with #[require(Camera)] rather than a standalone entity component

title: Non-collidable terrain walls sticking up everywhere near coastlines
description: Players spawning at the coastline (via find_coastline()) see many thin vertical walls rising from the ground in what appears to be flat ocean water. The walls have no collision because the physics system uses radial-only height-field collision. Root cause: the noise amplitude for all biomes is `roughness * 2000` metres. For ocean biomes (roughness≈0.08) the amplitude is ±160 m. Near coastlines the IDW-interpolated elevation can be as shallow as −50 to 0 m; noise then pushes individual columns above sea level, producing land spikes adjacent to water columns. Surface Nets correctly generates vertical faces between these columns, producing the visible walls.
resolution: GPU heightmap Phase 3 complete. Commit e48337a.
suspected_cause: Noise amplitude (roughness * 2000 m) exceeded shallow ocean depth near coastlines, allowing ocean terrain to breach sea level and generate land column spikes with non-collidable Surface Nets walls.

title: Blocky Minecraft-style terrain at high LOD levels
description: At LOD 2+ (voxel size 4–16+ metres), terrain rendered with Surface Nets produced large flat-topped rectangular blocks with perfect vertical walls — Minecraft-style geometry instead of smooth slopes. Root cause: terrain_density(depth) = clamp(0.5 + depth*0.5, 0.0, 1.0) saturates to exactly 0.0 or 1.0 for any voxel more than 1 m from the surface. At LOD 4 (16 m voxels), virtually every voxel was binary. With a binary SDF Surface Nets always placed vertices at exact voxel midpoints (t=0.5 in cell_vertex_offset), eliminating all sub-voxel gradient information and making every surface look like axis-aligned blocks.
resolution: GPU heightmap Phase 3 complete. Commit e48337a.
suspected_cause: terrain_density gradient width (±1 m) was independent of LOD voxel size, so high-LOD voxels (16 m) all saturated to binary SDF values.

title: Gray opaque ceiling above player when spawning at sea
description: The Surface Nets Y-axis edge loop in surface_nets_mesh ran `for j in 0..CSI` (CS=32). At j=CS-1=31, this emitted the edge (i,31,k)→(i,32,k), where corner j=32 is read from slices[2] (the +Y neighbour's j=0 face via generate_single_boundary_slice resampling). In the sea chunk, j=31 is AIR (r≈32015.5 > sea_level=32000) but the +Y neighbour's resampled j=0 row contains STONE for terrain columns where surface_r > 32016.5 nearby. This AIR→STONE sign change generated a downward-facing (-Y normal) stone face at the very top of the sea chunk, visible to the player below as a large gray opaque ceiling slab.
resolution: GPU heightmap Phase 3 complete. Commit e48337a.
suspected_cause: Off-by-one in Y-axis loop range: emitted the +Y cross-chunk seam instead of the correct -Y seam. The +Y chunk being AllAir (emitting no mesh) exposed the spurious face.

title: Phantom ceiling and walls in ocean — continent masking disabled
description: continent_enabled: false caused NoiseStack.sample() to return raw land noise (combined in [-1,1]) for ALL positions. Even ocean spawns had adjacent columns with combined > 0 (land above sea level). With height_scale=4000, even combined=0.001 gives 4m land bumps. Random land patches near ocean spawn created vertical walls (X/Z sign changes) and ceiling-like top faces of nearby land columns visible from sea level.
root_cause: continent_enabled: false in planet_config.ron noise block. The NoiseStack.sample() code path branches on this flag; when false it returns sample_land() for all positions regardless of ocean/land territory. Previous RENDER-006/007/008 fixes targeted meshing seams and Planetary mode code paths, not this Spherical mode terrain generation issue.
resolution: GPU heightmap Phase 3 complete. Commit e48337a.

title: GPU voxel shader does not implement planetary terrain (IDW) — FBM noise only
description: The GPU compute shader for voxel generation implements FBM noise terrain (matching the old SphericalTerrainGenerator). After the planetary refactor, the CPU path uses PlanetaryTerrainSampler (IDW from tectonic PlanetData). The GPU and CPU terrain algorithms are now mismatched. GPU parity tests (gpu_vs_cpu_surface_chunk_comparison, gpu_vs_cpu_parity_earth_scale_surface_chunk) are marked #[ignore] until the GPU shader is updated.
resolution: GPU heightmap Phase 3 complete. Commit e48337a.

title: Atmospheric sky gradient and sun disk not rendered — sky is a flat clear color
description: The in-game sky displays as a single flat color (the Bevy ClearColor). There is no atmospheric gradient from horizon to zenith, no visible sun disk, and no twilight coloring. The atmosphere shader infrastructure exists (SKY-001 and SKY-002 were resolved) but the sky rendering is not connected to the main game renderer path.
reproduction: Run the game and look at the sky. Sky is a uniform flat color at all times of day.
suspected_cause: The atmosphere/sky gradient shader exists but is either not spawned as a sky mesh entity in the main game path, or the shader parameters (sun direction, planet radius, atmosphere thickness) are not being fed from the live OrbitalState and PlanetConfig resources.
resolution: Implemented GPU sky dome: SkyMaterial (Bevy custom Material + WGSL Rayleigh scattering shader with sun disk) in src/lighting/sky_dome.rs and assets/shaders/sky_dome.wgsl. Sky dome sphere spawned at startup, anchored to camera each PostUpdate frame to handle floating origin. Uses AlphaMode::Blend to bypass depth prepass; reverse-Z z=0.0 trick ensures it renders at infinity. Old update_spherical_sky (flat ClearColor) removed.
notes: SKY-001 (gray/colorless world) and SKY-002 (black sky in planet mode) were both resolved but addressed different root causes. SKY-003 is the remaining issue: no visible gradient or sun disk.
src/gpu/renderer.rs has a HeadlessRenderParams struct with enable_stars: bool field, suggesting sky/star rendering is partially implemented for the headless path but not the live game.

title: No stars visible at night — night sky is solid dark color
description: At night, the sky is a solid dark color with no stars. There is no procedural star field generated for the system. Stars should be visible when the sun is below the horizon, fading in at dusk and out at dawn.
reproduction: Run the game and wait until night (or speed up time with time_scale). Night sky has no stars.
suspected_cause: No star field generation or rendering exists in the main game path. src/gpu/renderer.rs has an enable_stars: bool field on HeadlessRenderParams (currently false) that is not connected to the game renderer. A procedural star sphere driven by CelestialSystem data (or a seeded RNG from the system seed) needs to be implemented.
notes: Star positions should be consistent per system (driven by system seed or CelestialSystem data), not random each run.
Brightness should scale with sun elevation: fully visible at night, fading out around sunrise/sunset.
Could be rendered as a large inverted sphere with a star texture, or as point sprites, or as bright pixels in a sky shader.

title: Moons not rendered — Moon struct and moon_position() exist but no sky object is spawned
description: If the planetary system has moons, they should appear as visible objects in the sky when lit by the sun. The Moon struct and moon_position() function exist in src/planet/celestial.rs but no sky entity or billboard is spawned for them.
reproduction: Run the game with a system that has moons. No moon is visible in the sky.
suspected_cause: Moon rendering was never implemented. moon_position() computes the correct position but the result is not used to spawn a billboard, sprite, or sky mesh entity.
resolution: Implemented MoonBillboard quads in src/lighting/sky_dome.rs: spawn_moon_billboards (one-shot, spawns a Rectangle quad per moon using StandardMaterial unlit+emissive) and update_moon_positions (repositions each quad to VISUAL_MOON_DIST=50000m in the moon direction each frame, scales for correct apparent size, hides when below horizon). GameElapsedSeconds resource accumulates game-time for Keplerian orbit input.
notes: Lower priority than SKY-003 and SKY-004.
Moon brightness should depend on phase angle relative to sun (only lit side visible).
Should be blocked on SKY-003 being resolved first so the sky rendering pipeline is in place.

title: Narrow chunk seams still visible at LOD transitions and cube-face boundaries
description: Thin gaps remain between adjacent chunks where vertices do not align exactly. You can see terrain below through the seam. These are distinct from the resolved RENDER-004 (grid-like lat/lon seams): those were same-LOD seams fixed by surface nets padding. The remaining seams occur specifically at LOD transition boundaries (LOD 0 chunk adjacent to LOD 1 chunk) and at cube-face boundaries where two faces of the cubed-sphere meet.
reproduction: Look at terrain from above or at an angle, especially at medium distance where LOD transitions occur. Thin lines/gaps are visible between chunk edges.
suspected_cause: Different LOD levels produce mesh vertices at slightly different world positions (LOD seam stitching not implemented). The tangent-frame projection may also introduce sub-millimeter float precision errors at chunk boundaries. Cross-face chunk boundaries have more complex wrapping that may not be fully handled.
notes: surface_nets.rs comments mention: '1-voxel approximation cracks remain only at chunk corners (where 4 chunks meet along an edge)' — but user reports seams are still visible more broadly.
Check if seams are specifically at LOD boundaries (LOD 0 adjacent to LOD 1) vs same-LOD seams.
Check if seams are only at cube face boundaries or also within a single face.
A skirt/overlap approach (extending mesh slightly beyond chunk boundary) is a common workaround for LOD seams in voxel engines.

title: Overflow panic in mesh priority sort on teleport
description: Teleporting long distances caused a 'attempt to add with overflow' panic in the async mesh-priority computation (chunk_manager.rs:970). The sort key was i32 but the sum of class_penalty (up to 30M) + lod_penalty + dist (clamped to i32::MAX ≈ 2.1B) exceeded i32::MAX.
reproduction: Teleport to a distant coordinate (e.g. 70.8°S 125.0°E). Panic fires in Compute Task Pool thread during mesh scheduling.
suspected_cause: Priority key type i32 too narrow. dist was clamped to i32::MAX before being added to penalties, but the sum still overflowed.
resolution: Widened sort key and all penalty variables to i64. Removed the i32-range clamp on the distance squared term — i64 has ample headroom for planet-scale chunk coordinates. Fixed in next commit after 3649a77.
